title: Uva micrantha
taxonomy: Uva micrantha is classified within the family Vitaceae, which comprises various species of vines and climbing plants. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. The genus Uva is often associated with grapes and other related species. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Uva micrantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the Indian alkanet or Uva. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Uva micrantha include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Uva micrantha is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues.
